verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is synthesized as a precursor (pre-pro-TGF-β1) that is cleaved but remains non-covalently latent as the Large Latent Complex (LLC). The LLC is secreted and can be covalently tethered to the cell surface via LTBP isoforms crosslinked to ECM, or via LRRC32 (GARP) and LLTBP1 on the surface of Tregs and platelets, forming a stable, wash-resistant surface complex. Thus, the mature/latent TGF-β1 protein body reaches the outer leaflet of specific cell types (Tregs, platelets, tumor stroma) through stable covalent/wash-resistant anchoring to TM partners — not merely reversible receptor binding — qualifying as contextual/stable_surface_attachment.
